message:
So last week my kid asked me, "Mom, where's the flashlight?" and I realized I had no idea. Like, zero idea. I'd bought three of them over the past two years, confidently assuming they were "somewhere safe," and apparently "somewhere safe" means "scattered across three different junk drawers and one is missing batteries."

Turns out being prepared doesn't have to mean having everything perfectly organized in a color-coded emergency kit (though hey, no judgment if that's your style). Sometimes it just means knowing where the basics actually live, and not having to tear your house apart when you actually need them.

I spent 20 minutes last night doing a little inventory walk-through—found the flashlights, checked that our documents folder is still where I think it is, made sure we know where the water is. Nothing fancy. Just... knowing.

What's the one thing in your home you feel like you're *always* hunting for when you need it most? 😄
